ui.heroBody: Work through bite-size lessons. Each one runs real Rust code and asks you to predict its output or pick the correct answer. Free, no account, in your browser.

01_hello.title: Hello, world!
01_hello.description: Your first Rust program. The println! macro prints to standard output. Variables can be interpolated directly inside the format string.

q1.question: What does this program print? (Type the exact output.)

02_variables.title: Variables and mutability
02_variables.description: Variables are immutable by default. Add `mut` to allow reassignment. Type annotations are optional when the compiler can infer them.

q2.question: Given `let y: i32 = 10;` (without `mut`), which line below would still compile if added afterward?

03_ownership.title: Ownership and borrowing
03_ownership.description: Every value in Rust has exactly one owner. Passing a value by reference (&) borrows it without taking ownership. Once a value has been moved, the original binding can no longer be used.

q2.question: After `takes_ownership(owned)` is called, what happens if we try to use `owned` again?
